evidence: `joinClips` keeps four entry APIs (`createCanonicalJoinClipsTask`, `createMultiClipJoinTask`, `createLegacyJoinTask`, `createVideoEditJoinTask`) while `joinClipsCompatAdapter` introduces an additional mixed-shape compatibility API with overlapping fields and mode flags, creating parallel abstraction layers for the same operation.

`travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts` writes overlapping data into multiple payload sections (`orchestratorDetails`, `taskViewContract`, raw params), and `VideoTravelDetails.tsx` must resolve each display field via multi-source fallback chains, indicating no single authoritative contract.

Lightbox flow is split across multiple orchestration layers (`VideoLightbox.tsx` -> `useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` -> `LightboxLayout.tsx`) with large prop/controller pass-through surfaces and repeated assembly patterns mirrored again in inline edit UI paths.

Error-module migration is inconsistent in assigned tests: multiple files still mock `@/shared/lib/errorHandler` while assigned production files import `@/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError` (for example `src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts:15` vs `src/shared/lib/tasks/__tests__/imageGeneration.test.ts:58`, and `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts:12` vs `src/shared/lib/tasks/__tests__/joinClips.test.ts:51`).

The same stale mock path pattern appears in hook tests (`src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDarkMode.test.ts:17`, `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/usePlatformInstall.test.ts:4`, `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useGalleryPageState.test.ts:69`) while other assigned tests already use the new path (`src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useTaskCancellation.test.ts:17`, `src/shared/hooks/settings/__tests__/useAutoSaveSettings.test.ts:21`).

Within the same callback module, failure contracts differ by operation: `useDeleteSegmentHandler` throws via `handleAndRethrow` (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts:149-153`) while sibling operations catch and return `OperationResult` failures (`:215-221`, `:247-253`, `:287-293`).
error_consistency.issues_preventing_higher_score: Error-handling contracts are not consistently exercised or represented across sibling tests/callbacks, leaving failure behavior uneven and less predictable.

findings.summary: Join-clips task creation is split across overlapping APIs with a wide compatibility bag.
evidence: `JoinClipsTaskCompatParams` in `joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` mixes canonical (`clip_source`, `video_edit`) and legacy (`starting_video_path`, `video_edit_mode`) fields in one interface.

`joinClips.ts` already supports canonical mode routing and still exposes additional thin wrappers (`createMultiClipJoinTask`, `createLegacyJoinTask`, `createVideoEditJoinTask`) that mostly remap arguments into the same internal function.
Tests heavily target the compat API shape, reinforcing the duplicated abstraction path instead of converging call sites.
findings.suggestion: Define one discriminated input contract as the only public creation API, keep compat parsing at a single boundary module, and migrate tests/callers to canonical mode-specific inputs; then remove wrapper exports and legacy fields incrementally behind explicit deprecation metrics.

findings.summary: Travel payload contracts duplicate the same semantics across multiple envelopes.
evidence: `buildTravelBetweenImagesPayload` writes related values into raw orchestrator keys plus structured contracts (`taskViewContract`, orchestration metadata), preserving legacy key paths simultaneously.
`VideoTravelDetails.tsx` repeatedly resolves fields by trying `taskViewContract`, then `orchestratorDetails`, then `rawParams`, indicating readers cannot trust one canonical location.
`complete_task/generation.ts` builds snapshots from raw params, continuing dependence on broad payload introspection rather than a strict typed schema.
findings.suggestion: Promote a versioned canonical payload schema with explicit required fields for read paths, add one adapter for legacy payload ingestion, and simplify UI/backend readers to consume only canonical fields (with temporary fallback in one translator).

findings.summary: Lightbox and inline-edit orchestration rely on high-cost pass-through model layers.
evidence: `VideoLightbox.tsx` composes mode/environment/shared/edit/render models, then forwards assembled props to `LightboxLayout`, creating several intermediate abstraction layers with little domain reduction.
`useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` carries a large dependency/pass-through surface to build panel content and layout props, increasing indirection for simple UI changes.

Inline edit replicates similar orchestration and control surfaces (`useInlineEditState` + `InlineEditView`) while reusing low-level lightbox components, suggesting boundary duplication rather than a stable shared view model.
findings.suggestion: Collapse to one feature-level controller per mode (image/video/edit) that exposes a minimal, stable view contract to layout components, and extract shared edit-control primitives once for both lightbox and inline-edit paths to reduce pass-through seams.

findings.summary: Task tests mock a deprecated error module path, so error behavior is not consistently intercepted
evidence: Assigned task sources import `handleError` from `@/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError` (`imageGeneration.ts:15`, `joinClips.ts:12`), but assigned tests mock `@/shared/lib/errorHandler` (`imageGeneration.test.ts:58-60`, `joinClips.test.ts:51-53`, `individualTravelSegment.test.ts:29-31`).
Because the mocked module path differs from the imported module path, error-wrapping behavior in these tests is not uniformly controlled.
findings.suggestion: Standardize all assigned task tests to mock `@/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError` (or a shared test helper that centralizes the canonical error module path), then re-run assertions that depend on wrapped errors.

findings.summary: Sibling shot-editor callbacks mix throw-based and Result-based failures
evidence: `handleDeleteSegment` throws on failure via `handleAndRethrow` (`useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts:149-153`), while sibling operations (`runAddToShotOperation`, `runAddToShotWithoutPositionOperation`, `runCreateShotOperation`) catch errors and return `operationFailure` (`:215-221`, `:247-253`, `:287-293`).
This creates different caller obligations for closely related actions in the same hook surface: some paths require try/catch, others require `result.ok` branching.
findings.suggestion: Pick one failure contract for this callback family (prefer `OperationResult` for UI event handlers), refactor `handleDeleteSegment` to return `OperationResult` instead of throwing, and align callers such as segment-slot handlers to one consistent pattern.
